content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

August 22, 1990


Conny B. McCormack
Registrar of Voters
County of San Diego
5201 Ruffin Road, Suite 1
San Diego, CA 92123

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-90-497

Dear Ms. McCormack:

We have received your letter of July 25, 1990 in which you
seek advice regarding your duties and responsibilities as the
Registrar of Voters for San Diego County under the mass mailing
provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").1

QUESTION

Your office publishes and distributes a "Directory of Public
Officials" and a "Media Kit." In light of Proposition 732, are
you permitted to include a listing of the members of the county's
Board of Supervisors on the back cover of these publications?

CONCLUSION

You may include a roster listing the names of the members of
the county's Board of Supervisors on the back cover of these
publications, provided certain requirements are met.


_________________________

1 Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section
18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2, Di-
vision 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

2 Your inquiry also included reference to Proposition 68. The
mass mailing provisions at issue in your question are found in
Proposition 73; there are no comparable provisions in Proposition
68.


428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916)322-5660

File No. A-90-497
Page 2

FACTS

Your office biennially publishes a directory of public
officials. The directory includes federal and state offices and
officeholders, members of the legislature who represent the
county, county and incorporated city offices, and school and
special districts. Other information concerning election dates,
county facts, and addresses and telephone numbers of general
interest are also provided.

Your office also publishes a media kit which is produced and
distributed prior to each "major" election. The media kit con-
tains information concerning election statistics, campaign disclo-
sure, sample ballots, polls location, absentee voting, election
night vote tabulation, ballot propositions, and an election
handbook.

Attached to your letter requesting advice were copies of the
media kit and directory of public officials.

ANALYSIS

Section 82041.5 defines a mass mailing as more than 200 sub-
stantially similar pieces of mail. Section 89001 prohibits a
newsletter or other mass mailing from being sent at public
expense.

Regulation 18901 (copy enclosed) implements Section 89001 and
clarifies that the purpose of the mass mailing restriction is to
prevent elected officers from using public funds to further their
candidacy for reelection by sending out newsletters and mass mass
mailings. (Krannawitter Advice Letter, No. A-90-300, Symkowick
Advice Letter, No. I-88-399, copies enclosed.)

Regulation 18901(b)(1) provides in part that a newsletter or
other mass mailing is not prohibited by Section 89001 if

... the elected officer's name appears ... in a
roster listing containing the names of all elected
officers of the agency. In any such items, the
names of all elected officers must appear in the
same type size, typeface, type color, and location.
Such item may not include the elected officer's
photograph, signature, or any other reference to
the elected officer, except as specifically
permitted in subdivision (b)(1) or elsewhere in
this regulation.

Regulation 18901(b)(1).

A roster listing that contains the names of the elected mem-
bers of the board of supervisors in the same typeface, size, and
location would therefore appear to be in compliance with the mass

File No. A-90-497
Page 3


mailing provisions of the Act.  (Steffan Advice Letter, No.
A 90 033, Biondo Advice Letter, No. A-89-022, copies enclosed.)
This would be equally applicable to both the media kit and the
directory of officials.

     I trust this letter has provided you with the guidance you
requested. If you have any further questions regarding this mat-
ter, please contact me at (916) 322-5901.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        Scott Hallabrin
                                        Acting General Counsel

                                   By:  Jonathan S. Rothman
                                        Counsel, Legal Division

SH:JSR:plh

Enclosures
